nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.label_negative: Manual drafting and editing without AI
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly use it for drafting emails or proofreading/editing emails

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.grounding_span: I would ask it something like, 'Give me a way to incorporate the 4 W's into a lesson on sentences'

nodes.label: AI energy consumption
nodes.grounding_span: It's not so much the specific task but how high or low energy I'm feeling on a particular day

nodes.label: AI independence in task completion
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to give it tasks to complete independently, but modify its response to suit the student

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Anything I've asked it to do, it's done well enough that I could use it or adapt it slightly

nodes.label: Irreplaceable human role in special education
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing human role in special education
nodes.grounding_span: Computer AI can't accompany a student to a specials class or modify their behavior - that has to come from a human

nodes.label: AI as information provider vs teacher
nodes.grounding_span: teachers are using AI but in general students are expected not to

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: Those times I am more mentally and emotionally drained and ready for a break

nodes.label: Personalized student support
nodes.grounding_span: I generally modify them for the specific student, since they all have different abilities

nodes.label: Professional autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I've thought about it a bit, but there will always be a need for a role like mine

nodes.label: Workplace harmony and approval
nodes.grounding_span: I can see some conflict rearing its head over that in the coming years

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: In general I feel satisfied with it

nodes.label: Preference for self-creation on high-energy days
nodes.grounding_span: On days when behaviors are low, I tend to have more energy to put into tasks myself

nodes.label: Concern about teacher-student AI policy conflict
nodes.grounding_span: I can see some conflict rearing its head over that in the coming years

nodes.label: Energy-dependent task delegation — adjusts AI use based on mental/emotional state
nodes.grounding_span: It's not so much the specific task but how high or low energy I'm feeling on a particular day

nodes.label: Selective delegation — prefers AI for content creation, retains control over structure
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to give it tasks to complete independently, but modify its response to suit the student
